original_filename: Sharma et al. (2026) - Who’s in Charge Disempowerment Patterns.pdf

decisions.reason: Sharma et al. (2026) disempowerment patterns. High-priority for next session.

Re-triggered from DEFER. Slug shortened to sharma-disempowerment-patterns-2026. First large-scale empirical analysis of human disempowerment patterns in real-world AI assistant interactions (1.5M Claude.ai conversations). HIGH relevance, HIGH quality.

Source distillation + 1 NEW concept (situational-disempowerment) + 6 UPDATE proposals (agency, sycophancy, social-sycophancy, delusional-spiraling, cognitive-surrender, belief-offloading, ai-loneliness-effect).
Overall READY. AI-failure checklist clean. All 8 drafts approved across all 3 lenses.
Stage 4.5 caught 1 missing_related (moral-crumple-zone in Contradicts/Extends); fixed; second-pass clean.

Source distillation moved to sources/. New concept situational-disempowerment moved to concepts/. 6 UPDATEs applied to existing concept entries. sync-source-links + build-index + update_readme_counts run successfully. KB total 125→127.
